title: Comforting Chinese Tofu Bao
description: A nutty and pillowy Chinese dish from Zhejiang, featuring mayonnaise and smoke-hot flavors, all in a vegan-friendly package.

ingredients.name: firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, smoked paprika, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil.
Add the cubed tofu to the bowl and toss to coat with the mayonnaise mixture.
Spread the tofu mixture on a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes.
While the tofu is baking, toast the bao buns by steaming them for 5 minutes.
Assemble the bao by spreading a layer of the tofu mixture on the bottom bun, followed by a slice of pickled ginger, a few slices of scallion, and a drizzle of sriracha.
Top with the top bun and serve immediately.
Garnish with additional scallions and pickled ginger if desired.
Serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a simple green salad for a well-rounded meal.
